source: Node tests and Playwright Chromium interaction, visual, accessibility, touch, and performance suites

observed_contracts.component_presentation: Product, Architecture, Research, Business, and Review renderers explicitly report the Components presented by the active Frame, mode, or filter; the v2 compatibility renderer preserves active-Frame fallback behavior.
observed_contracts.architecture_direction: Runtime validation rejects unresolved or unordered Scenario Paths, every rendered edge has a directed marker, and both Current and Proposed modes expose labeled Scenario Path Start and End nodes plus a readable toolbar summary.
observed_contracts.resizing: The shared Canvas/Feedback separator and Architecture viewport separator support pointer and keyboard operation, bounded ARIA values, persistence, mobile stacking, touch hit geometry, and Canvas-width-aware Workspace reflow.

residual_risks: Manual screenshot inspection and browser automation covered Chromium; Firefox and WebKit were not manually exercised.
Coarse-pointer layout was verified with Chromium touch emulation rather than a physical tablet.
No real screen-reader speech or touch assistive technology session was exercised.
